channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Jordan Stolz opens his bid for 4 golds by winning the 1,000 meters in speedskating
item.description: Stolz received his gold for winning the men's 1,000 meters at the Milan Cortina Games in an Olympic-record time thanks to a blistering closing stretch...

item.title: Free speech lawsuits mount after Charlie Kirk assassination
item.description: Months after the killing of Charlie Kirk, a growing number of lawsuits by people claim they were illegally punished, fired and even arrested for makin...

item.title: Push for Body Cameras for D.H.S. Underscores Trump Administration’s Shift
item.description: Immigration enforcement agencies have received tens of millions in funding for body camera programs, which the Trump administration proposed cutting —...

item.title: How to hone your 'friendship intuition'
item.description: Friendship expert Kat Vellos shares tips on how to make a new friendship stick, including what to do together, how often to hang out — and what to do ...

item.title: Satellite Pictures Show Snow And Ice Across North America
item.description: For much of North America, the last month has been an icy, frozen trudge. But from the right vantage point, it had a certain beauty.

item.title: Immigration Officials Set to Testify Before Senate Panel
item.description: The hearing comes two days after the officials declined to answer questions from House members about the fatal shootings of Alex Pretti and Renee Good...

item.title: Ukrainian Olympian Is Disqualified Over Helmet With Images of War Dead
item.description: Olympic officials had told Vladyslav Heraskevych that the helmet, featuring images of Ukrainian athletes killed in the war with Russia, violated the G...

item.title: Anti-Drone Laser Is Said to Have Caused El Paso Airport Chaos, and Bondi Refuses to Apologize to Epstein Victims
item.description: Plus, how the ultra-rich are changing the travel industry.

item.title: 6 GOP reps defy Trump to block Canadian tariffs. And, student loan defaults rise
item.description: Six House Republicans joined Democrats to successfully block Trump's Canadian tariffs. And, roughly a million borrowers defaulted on their federal stu...

item.title: Top immigration officials to testify before Senate as a potential DHS shutdown looms
item.description: The leaders of ICE, Customs and Border Protection and and U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services will appear for their second oversight hearing thi...

item.title: These Ukrainian Drones Don’t Just Kill. They Deliver Oatmeal Cookies.
item.description: In a war where drones are synonymous with destruction, some are also used to drop care packages to Ukraine’s frontline soldiers.

item.title: NATO Remains Vital to U.S. Security, Ex-Ambassadors and Generals Say
item.description: Despite doubts over Washington’s commitment to European security, the alliance enhances American national interests, a bipartisan group of 16 high-lev...

item.title: Tumbler Ridge Shooting: What to Know About the Victims and Suspect
item.description: The police identified an 18-year-old as the suspect believed to have fatally shot eight people in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, before killing hers...

item.title: Ukrainian Olympian Is Disqualified Over Helmet With Images of War Dead
item.description: Olympic officials had told Vladyslav Heraskevych that the helmet, featuring images of Ukrainian athletes killed in the war with Russia, violated the G...

item.title: Across Italy, the Winter Olympic Vibes Are Very Different
item.description: In Milan, the hub of the Winter Games, the atmosphere seems subdued, while the small towns hosting mountain events are seeing enthusiastic crowds.
